key_insight: The paper gives a unified convergence framework for decentralized stochastic subgradient methods under compressed communication, even for nonsmooth nonconvex objectives without Clarke regularity.
engineering_relevance: It informs how to design communication-efficient distributed optimization algorithms for large-scale edge, sensor, and multi-agent systems where bandwidth is limited.
method: Builds a general analysis framework covering unbiased compression and contractive compression with error compensation.
Links consensus-error and averaged-iterate dynamics to continuous-time differential inclusions to prove global convergence.
Develops new compressed decentralized methods using sign-based regularization and gradient-tracking momentum.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary numerical experiments that empirically validate the theory and illustrate the communication-accuracy trade-off.
watch_item: The numerical evidence is only preliminary, so practical performance may depend strongly on the compression scheme, step-size choices, and network topology.
paper_title: Decentralized Stochastic Subgradient-type Methods with Communication Compression for Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
